  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance

  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

Fix Gradle SIGSEGV crash in Docker builds on ARM (Apple Silicon)

Switch build stage from eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine to eclipse-temurin:21-jdk
to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify connection path in architecture diagram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Update README and CLAUDE.md for new stack

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Go migration v2 — unified Dolt data layer, CR fixes (#25)

* feat: Go migration v2 — unified Dolt data layer, 15-batch PDLC

Complete rewrite of hookwise from TypeScript to Go with Dolt embedded
as the unified, version-controlled SQL data layer.

What changed:
- TypeScript → Go (single binary, 65µs dispatch latency)
- SQLite + JSON files → Dolt embedded (branch, diff, commit, rollback)
- Child process daemon → goroutine-based feed polling
- React/Ink CLI → Cobra CLI (Python TUI preserved)

Key packages:
- internal/core: dispatcher, config, guards, types, state utilities
- internal/analytics: Dolt-backed session/event tracking
- internal/feeds: goroutine-per-feed daemon with 8 built-in producers
- internal/bridge: Go→Python TUI cache bridge (FlattenForTUI)
- internal/notifications: trigger-based notification platform
- internal/upgrade: TypeScript→Go data migration tool
- pkg/hookwise/testing: GuardTester + HookRunner testing API

Testing:
- 91% coverage (testing API), 82.5% (feeds)
- 33 contract test fixtures across 13 event types
- 10 integration tests, 10 benchmarks
- 8 holdout scenarios all PASS

PDLC: 15 batches, all DONE+CRITICS (ADVOCATE + SKEPTIC)
Final Validator: PASS_WARN (both perspectives)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify — fix 15 issues from code review agents

Code Reuse (4 HIGH fixed):
- Remove exact duplicate `mapToHandlerResult` (use `actionToResult`)
- Remove dead types: CostState, AuthorshipEntry, ToolBreakdownEntry,
  DailySummary, StatsResult from core/types.go (superseded by analytics)
- Consolidate triplicated `parseTimeFlex` into core.ParseTimeFlex

Code Quality (2 HIGH fixed):
- SafeDispatch: use named returns so panic recovery sets fail-open result
- Add action constants (ActionBlock/Warn/Confirm/Allow) and phase
  constants (PhaseGuard/Context/SideEffect), replace all raw strings

Efficiency (4 P0/P1 fixed):
- payloadToMap: direct struct→map instead of JSON marshal/unmarshal
- Glob pattern caching via sync.Map (avoid recompilation per dispatch)
- Regex caching via sync.Map for `matches` operator
- Hoist inferPhase/phaseOrder maps to package level (avoid per-call alloc)

Other:
- Consolidate fireSideEffects/FireSideEffectsSync into shared runSideEffects
- Replace custom minInt with Go 1.21+ builtin min()
- Replace log.Printf with core.Logger() in feeds/polling.go

Net: -55 lines, 11 files changed. All 9 packages pass with -race.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 30 CodeRabbit review comments from PR #25

Code fixes (22 items):
- stdin.go: add io.LimitReader(10MB) and improve malformed JSON warning
- main.go: add 50ms grace period for side-effect goroutines before os.Exit
- main.go: log MarkSurfaced errors instead of silently swallowing
- custom.go: use Setpgid + process group kill for child processes
- constants.go: warn on TempDir fallback when UserHomeDir unavailable
- producer.go: add nil-map guard in Registry.Register
- migration.go: return error instead of time.Now() fallback on parse failure
- migration.go: defer Analytics handle creation to avoid nil DB in dry-run
- builtin.go: add "source":"placeholder" marker to all stub producers
- config.go: check type assertion result on InterpolateEnvVars
- dispatcher.go: use json.Marshal for fallback JSON string escaping
- state.go: log silent JSON unmarshal errors in coaching/cost state
- state.go: log time parse fallback in ReadFeedCache
- state.go: add TTL check to ReadAllFeedCache (consistent with ReadFeedCache)
- bridge.go: add type validation for ttl_seconds field
- producers.go: escape SQL LIKE wildcards in content substring
- daemon.go: check f.Close() error after PID file write

Test fixes (5 items):
- dispatcher_test.go: replace no-op assert.True(t, true) with require.NotPanics
- state_test.go: capture expected date before DB read (midnight race fix)
- cli_test.go: strengthen ldflags test to assert exact version format
- contract_test.go: fix operator extraction to match ops without spaces
- integration_test.go: replace time.Sleep with require.Eventually polling
- migration_test.go: fix fmtID to use fmt.Sprintf (supports >26 values)

Doc updates (3 items):
- boundaries.md: rewrite TypeScript paths to Go paths
- tech-stack.md: rewrite TS/Node stack to Go/Dolt
- roadmap.md: update pre-migration stack references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): remove CGO cross-compile steps, add timeout

Dolt's gozstd dependency requires CGO, which needs a platform-specific
C toolchain unavailable on GitHub Actions Linux runners. The darwin/arm64
cross-compile step fails with undefined symbols from the zstd C library.

Also adds timeout-minutes: 15 to prevent runaway builds (CR thread #1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove returnAll from agent-facing search tools to prevent token spikes (#2272) (#2273)

The agent was triggering returnAll too aggressively, causing massive token
usage. This removes returnAll from the tool schema, planning prompt, and
all search paths:

- Remove returnAll from localSearch Zod schema (no longer agent-settable)
- Remove OR stacking that expanded limits when time range or tags present
- Maintain similarity score floor at 0.1 (was dropping to 0.0)
- Remove returnAll from CopilotPlusChainRunner planning prompt and extraction
- Clean up agent prompt in builtinTools.ts
- All search paths now use DEFAULT_MAX_SOURCE_CHUNKS (30) consistently

commit.message:
fix: 合格者カウント漏れとアクティブ判定の修正

[合格者75→81問題]
42 APIでピシン合格後に本科(cursus_42)へ移行すると
piscine cursusエントリが削除される場合がある。
graduated_loginsにいてもstudentsに存在しない学生を
cursus42のデータで補完して passed_count を正確に集計する。

[アクティブ116→125問題]
ピシン終了後も「直近7日間」で判定していたため
最終週に来なかった学生が除外されていた。
ピシン終了後は全ピシン期間（02/02〜02/27）を対象にして
一度でも1h以上来た学生をアクティブ母集団とする。

feat: macOS sidebar-only vibrancy (NSVisualEffectView, macOS 15+) (#10)

* fix: reset cursor on mouse exit + remap Cmd+Delete to Ctrl+U

- Capture widget's mouse_interaction now returns default interaction
  when the cursor is outside the terminal bounds, preventing the
  text/I-beam cursor from sticking when the mouse moves to the sidebar.

- Cmd+Delete (forward-delete key) remapped from DeleteLineRight (Ctrl+K)
  to DeleteLineLeft (Ctrl+U), matching macOS system-wide Cmd+Delete
  behaviour (delete to line start).

- Removed unreachable ShortcutAction::DeleteLineRight variant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: macOS sidebar-only vibrancy via NSVisualEffectView ObjC FFI

Insert an NSVisualEffectView (Sidebar material, BehindWindow blending,
Active state) at index 0 of the contentView on first update, covering
only the sidebar width. The Metal layer renders transparent pixels for
the sidebar (container background=None) so the NSVisualEffect composites
with the desktop. Terminal area retains its opaque #1e1e1e background,
preventing grey bleed.

- apply_sidebar_vibrancy(sidebar_width) → bool, called once on first update
- macos_major_version() via sysctl for graceful fallback on < macOS 15
- NSViewHeightSizable autoresize mask: sidebar effect tracks window height
- win.transparent=true enabled (required for per-pixel Metal alpha)
- content_area container gets explicit terminal_bg background to cover
  pane spacing gaps
- vibrancy_state: Option<bool> tracks attempt/success per session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: CodeRabbit review items for sidebar vibrancy PR

- Remove dead code: Message::DeleteLineRight variant and handler were
  unreachable (ShortcutAction::DeleteLineRight removed, Cmd+Del now maps
  to DeleteLineLeft only)
- sysctlbyname: check return value and bail with 0 on error instead of
  parsing potentially-uninitialized buf
- NSVisualEffectView setters: replace magic numbers (7,1,0,16) with
  named constants NS_VISUAL_EFFECT_MATERIAL_SIDEBAR,
  NS_VISUAL_EFFECT_STATE_ACTIVE, NS_VISUAL_EFFECT_BLENDING_BEHIND_WINDOW,
  NS_VIEW_HEIGHT_SIZABLE

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: flirt tendency slider not draggable due to missing onValueChange

Controlled Radix slider needs onValueChange for visual feedback during drag.
Added optimistic local state update while dragging, persists on release.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Poll for re-correction completion instead of using fixed 15s timeout

The backend runs re-correction asynchronously and responds immediately.
With many answers (500+), correction takes ~2.5 minutes. The old 15s
timeout was too short, leaving results showing null points. Now polls
every 5s until all answers have points_earned, with a 5-minute safety
timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase AI token budgets to handle large plant selections

SubgridCells for herbs/flowers are ~4x the JSON size of plain PlantCells,
so a 16x16 garden with 20 plants was hitting the 3500-token ceiling and
throwing "Garden is too large". New budgets:
  ≤16 cells: 2000  ≤32 cells: 3500  ≤64 cells: 6000  >64 cells: 10000
(+1500 for careCalendar on Harvest plans)

commit.message:
getTextSnapshot invokes user constructor; fix createEmptyMovieClip context

Two fixes:

1. getTextSnapshot() now invokes _global.TextSnapshot as a constructor
   (new TextSnapshot(mc)) instead of always creating a built-in object.
   If the user overrides _global.TextSnapshot, their constructor is called
   with the MovieClip as the first argument. Falls back to built-in
   TextSnapshot for stub constructors.

2. createEmptyMovieClip via CallFunction now uses g_current_context
   instead of hardcoded root_movieclip, so it works correctly when
   called from child MC scripts.

Test: movieclip_gettextsnapshot 112/112 PASS (was 6/112)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: proton vpn/mail cross-platform, cursor nixpkg, slack, bridge removal

Cursor:
- Linux: switch from AppImage download to pkgs.code-cursor (nixpkgs, v2.5.26)

Slack:
- New module system/shared/app/communication/slack.nix
- macOS: Homebrew cask; Linux: pkgs.slack

Proton Mail:
- Move from system/darwin/app/ to system/shared/app/productivity/
- Linux: add pkgs.protonmail-desktop (nixpkgs, v1.12.1)

Proton VPN:
- Re-enable pkgs.protonvpn-gui for Linux (build was broken, now fixed)
- Add pkgs.proton-vpn-cli alongside GUI
- Auto-login via mkActivationScript: user.proton.username + user.proton.password
- Supports both values as secrets; alphabetical field ordering guarantees
  $PROTON_PASSWORD is set before the username command block runs

Proton Mail Bridge:
- Remove system/shared/family/linux/app/productivity/protonmail-bridge.nix
- Remove security.protonBridgePassword from user-schema.nix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: skip remote state on new projects to unblock plan-all (#53)

When plan-all.sh runs on a brand-new project, custom-stack-provision
fails because terraform_remote_state tries to read cloud-provision's
state from S3 before it exists. This adds auto-detection: if
cloud-provision's plan JSON shows no prior_state resources, a temp
skip_remote_state.auto.tfvars.json is written so custom-stack-provision
skips the data source and falls back to variable defaults.

Closes luthersystems/reliable#597

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: write CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md to root, correct skill paths (#78)

* fix: write CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md to workspace root, use correct skill paths

CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md were being written inside .agents/ instead of the
workspace root directory. Skills for Claude Code agents were also placed in
.agents/skills/ instead of .claude/skills/. This fix:

- Writes CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md directly to workspace root
- Places skills in .claude/skills/ for Claude Code agents
- Places skills in .agents/skills/ for all other agents (opencode, codex)
- Updates skill link paths in generated markdown to match new locations
- Adds .claude/skills/ to syncSkillsFromWorkspace scan directories
- Removes creation of .agents/ dir for documentation files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: inject memory file read instruction into agent system prompts

Claude Code agents spawned by 20x don't auto-read CLAUDE.md from the
workspace since they're started by an external orchestrator, not the CLI.
This adds a system prompt instruction telling the agent to read its memory
file at session start:

- Claude Code agents: instructed to read CLAUDE.md
- Other agents (opencode, codex): instructed to read AGENTS.md

The instruction is appended in all three session config builders
(buildSessionConfig, startAdapterSession, resumeAdapterSession) to ensure
coverage across new sessions, resumed sessions, and ongoing operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move memory file instruction from system prompt to initial user message

System prompt instructions were being ignored by agents spawned via the 20x
orchestrator. Instead, prepend the instruction to the initial user message
which is more reliably followed:

- Removed buildMemoryFileInstruction and all 3 system prompt injections
- Added getMemoryFileName(agentId) helper (returns CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md)
- Prepend "read <file>" instruction to the initial user prompt in
  startAdapterSession, so it's part of the first user message
- Updated tests accordingly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move memory file instruction to end of prompt, use "Work on task:"

- Memory file read instruction now appended at the bottom of the initial
  user message instead of prepended at the top
- Changed "Working on task" to "Work on task:" for clearer directive tone

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(#131): retry WebSerial::init() in loop until WiFi connects

WebSerial::init() was only called once in setup(), but WiFi.begin()
is non-blocking — WiFi was still CONNECTING when init ran, so the
web server silently never started. Moving init() into loop() lets
it retry each iteration (no-op once started).
